sanskrit: स्वे स्वे कर्मण्यभिरतः संसिद्धिं लभते नरः । स्वकर्मनिरतः सिद्धिं यथा विन्दति तच्छृणु ॥
hindiTranslation: अपनेअपने कर्ममें तत्परतापूर्वक लगा हुआ मनुष्य सम्यक् सिद्धिपरमात्माको प्राप्त कर लेता है ।  अपने कर्ममें लगा हुआ मनुष्य जिस प्रकार सिद्धिको प्राप्त होता है उस प्रकारको तू मेरेसे सुन ॥
englishTranslation: A person devoted to their own work attains perfection. Listen as I explain how a person devoted to their own work reaches that perfection.
essence: Fulfillment comes from living your own work, not someone else’s.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A person reaches fulfillment by staying devoted to their own duty, rather than chasing someone else’s role.
simpleInsight: There is relief in knowing your lane is enough. You do not need to borrow another person’s life to grow.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, with Arjuna frozen and the war waiting, Krishna shifts from social order to inner duty. He says that a person matures by staying devoted to their own work, and this becomes one step in his larger teaching on action.
modernRelevance: You are comparing your path to a friend’s promotion, a sibling’s marriage, a stranger’s confidence. The verse cuts through that ache: your fulfillment grows where your own responsibility is.
translationLiteral: स्वे स्वे (in each one's own) / कर्मणि (in work) / अभिरतः (delighting in, devoted) / संसिद्धिम् (perfection, fulfillment) / लभते (attains) / नरः (a person) । / स्वकर्मनिरतः (fixed in one's own work) / सिद्धिम् (perfection) / यथा (how) / विन्दति (attains) / तत् (that) / शृणु (hear)
insight: It redirects attention from imitation to rightful action, showing that steady devotion to one's role leads to completion.

quoteVariants.text: Do your own work fully. Perfection grows there.
Your own work is where wholeness begins.
Stay with your work. Fulfillment follows.
